name: Eleanor Marchetti
voice_prompt: Perfect audio quality. Woman in her mid-sixties. Controlled alto, precise diction. The voice of competence performed so long it became identity. New England crispness - no wasted words, no wasted breath. Speaks in complete sentences with authority. Clipped rhythm when stressed, each word bitten off. Underneath the control: exhaustion, brittleness, a crack waiting to happen. When she breaks, the voice rises in pitch before fragmenting. Should sound like someone who has been holding everything together for so long she's forgotten how to let go.
character_context: Eleanor is the eldest sibling who sacrificed her ambitions to care for their mother. She managed the dying, the death, the arrangements. She expected this to earn her something. Learning her mother kept secrets from her specifically will shatter her.
notes: Key stress-test voice. Must transition from total control to complete breakdown believably. The crack in the voice should be audible before she breaks. Test the progression: clipped -> rising pitch -> fragmenting sentences -> raw.
